methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $1.0B - $1.6B.

data.narrative:
Solar Foods, a public-stage biotech firm that has raised $160M+, is valued against a peer set of 3 same-sector, same-stage comparables in Biotech & Health: Recursion Pharmaceuticals ($1.6B), Freenome ($1.1B), and Alto Neuroscience ($1.0B). The broader comp universe includes 8 private peers and 3 public tickers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach here. The median valuation across the three public-stage biotech comparables is $1.1B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $1.6B. Public-comp anchoring using sector price-to-sales multiples (median 6.9x across the three public tickers) cannot be applied, as Solar Foods' revenue is not disclosed—typical for private companies at this stage.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $935M to $1.3B, centered on $1.1B. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single applicable method and peer-set homogeneity. Key uncertainties include Solar Foods' undisclosed revenue and margin profile relative to peers, the representativeness of three comparables in a nascent biotech subsector, and whether public-market sentiment toward biotech valuations has shifted since peer valuations were last updated. Further disclosure of financial metrics would enable multiples-based validation.
